typed_components.excerpt:
***By the end of this module, the learner will be able to:***
			- ***MO 1.1:** Definition of the innovation mindset, why it is important and common misconceptions.*

typed_components.label: Course 1 Goal
typed_components.excerpt: To equip learners with the foundational mindsets and skills to identify, validate, and articulate a compelling opportunity for innovation within their clinical environment.

typed_components.label: Case for Change - Chapter Intro
typed_components.excerpt: Introduction: Healthcare is undergoing structural transformation. Physician roles, practice models, and organizational expectations are shifting rapidly, yet professional preparation has not evolved at the same pace.

typed_components.label: YouTube: Healthcare - Supply and Demand
typed_components.excerpt: - [Healthcare - Supply and Demand.  Why do healthcare markets fail?](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GjRAHuHIOD0)

typed_components.excerpt: - Slide 1: Healthcare Waste:

typed_components.label: Slide 1 Narration - JAMA waste estimate
typed_components.excerpt: - estimated cost of waste in the US health care system ranged from $760 billion to $935 billion, accounting for approximately 25% of total health care spending, and the projected potential savings

typed_components.label: JAMA 2019 Waste in US Health Care System
typed_components.excerpt: (Shrank WH, Rogstad TL, Parekh N. Waste in the US Health Care System: Estimated Costs and Potential for Savings. JAMA. 2019;322(15):1501–1509. doi:10.1001/jama.2019.13978)

typed_components.label: PubMed link - JAMA waste paper
typed_components.excerpt: - [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31589283/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31589283/)

typed_components.label: Commonwealth Fund / McKinsey - Mirror, Mirror (2024)
typed_components.excerpt: Approximately 25% of total U.S. healthcare spending (nearly $1 trillion) goes toward administrative costs—not patient care. The U.S. healthcare system currently ranks last among high-income nations

typed_components.label: Slide 3 - Rising Burden of Burnout
typed_components.excerpt: - Slide 3: The Rising Burden of Burnout

typed_components.label: Slide 3 Narration - framing quote
typed_components.excerpt: "Let's look at the data. Reports like the Medscape National Physician Burnout & Suicide Report show rising levels of burnout. CMS data shows healthcare spending approaching 20% of GDP.

typed_components.label: Slide 3 Narration - burnout rates and drivers
typed_components.excerpt: In 2024, 49% to 54.5% of physicians reported symptoms of burnout. Crucially, the top driver is not "too many patients," but "bureaucratic tasks" (charting/paperwork), which consistently rank as the #1 stressor.

typed_components.label: Medscape/AMA burnout reports
typed_components.excerpt: (Medscape Physician Burnout & Depression Report (2024) / AMA National Physician Comparison Report)

provisional_los.statement: Define the innovation mindset and identify common misconceptions about it.

source_refs.quoted_span: - ***MO 1.1:** Definition of the innovation mindset, why it is important and common misconceptions.*

provisional_los.statement: Analyze how psychological safety and fixed/growth mindsets affect team performance, patient safety, and innovation.

source_refs.quoted_span: - ***MO 1.2:** Analyze the impact of psychological safety and fixed/growth mindsets on clinical team performance, patient safety, and innovation.*

provisional_los.statement: Apply the Intelligent Failure framework to distinguish between blameworthy and praiseworthy failures in clinical settings.

source_refs.quoted_span: - ***MO 1.3:** Apply the "Intelligent Failure" framework to distinguish between blameworthy and praiseworthy failures in a clinical setting.*

provisional_los.statement: Evaluate your personal innovation mindset, goals, and environment using a structured SWOT analysis.

source_refs.quoted_span: - ***MO 1.4:** Evaluate one's personal innovation mindset, goals, and professional environment through a structured SWOT analysis.*

provisional_los.statement: Create an initial innovation portfolio sourced from experience, goals, and daily clinical observation.

source_refs.quoted_span: - ***MO 1.5:** Create an initial innovation portfolio by sourcing ideas from experience, future goals, and daily clinical observation.*

provisional_los.statement: Quantify the estimated cost and proportion of waste in the US health care system using published data.

source_refs.quoted_span: estimated cost of waste in the US health care system ranged from $760 billion to $935 billion, accounting for approximately 25% of total health care spending

provisional_los.statement: Identify bureaucratic tasks as a leading driver of physician burnout based on recent reports.

source_refs.quoted_span: the top driver is not "too many patients," but "bureaucratic tasks" (charting/paperwork), which consistently rank as the #1 stressor.

provisional_los.statement: Describe the shift from sickcare to proactive, population-focused healthcare models and its economic drivers.

source_refs.quoted_span: pushing healthcare toward more proactive, population-focused models.

provisional_los.statement: Articulate why healthcare is at an inflection point requiring new models of leadership and innovation.

source_refs.quoted_span: healthcare is not simply changing, but positioned at an inflection point that requires new models of leadership and innovation.

provisional_los.statement: Describe the scale of US administrative health care costs and their impact on efficiency and equity rankings.

source_refs.quoted_span: Approximately 25% of total U.S. healthcare spending (nearly $1 trillion) goes toward administrative costs—not patient care.

dissents.marcus_position: typed src-001-c012 (Slide 3) as narration; a defensible alternative reading is slide

resolved_ref.title: Waste in the US Health Care System

pedagogy_annotations.rationale: Motivates need for innovation by explaining the sector’s inflection point (lo-g0-009), priming learners to understand why new models are required.
Introduces key figures on waste, administrative costs, and burnout for recall, supporting quantification and recognition (lo-g0-006, lo-g0-010, lo-g0-007).
Synthesizes and analyzes how admin burden and waste contribute to inefficiency and burnout, aligning with lo-g0-010, lo-g0-007, and lo-g0-006.
Defines the shift from sickcare to proactive, population-focused care and frames the inflection point (lo-g0-008, lo-g0-009).
Analyzes economic and policy drivers to synthesize why healthcare is at an inflection point (lo-g0-009).
Applies the proactive-care model in a concrete example to show how the shift operates in practice (lo-g0-008).
Defines innovation mindset, psychological safety, and intelligent failure distinctions to build foundational understanding (lo-g0-001, lo-g0-002, lo-g0-003).
Applies concepts through clinical examples to distinguish praiseworthy vs blameworthy failures and team impacts (lo-g0-003, lo-g0-002, lo-g0-001).
Assesses learners’ ability to analyze and recall data and concepts across waste/admin costs, proactive care, inflection point, and mindset/failure frameworks.
